zh.lifeSummary: 當Alard le Fleming II於1220年出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾薩培頓時，他的父親Henry le Fleming I21歲，母親Joan16歲。他於大約1240年與Emma Maunsell在英國英格蘭素可普爾波洛結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1263年在英國英格蘭素可普爾波洛享年43歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alard le Fleming II于1220年出生在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾薩培頓时，他的父亲Henry le Fleming I21岁，母亲Joan16岁。他于大约1240年与Emma Maunsell在英国英格兰素可普爾波洛结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1263年在英国英格兰素可普爾波洛享年43岁，埋葬在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Fleming
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, from Middle English fleming. The word reflects a Norman French form of Old French flamanc ‘Fleming’, from the stem flam- + the ancient Germanic suffix -ing. In the Middle Ages there was considerable commercial intercourse between England and the Netherlands, particularly in the wool trade, and many Flemish weavers and dyers settled in England. This surname is also common in south and east Scotland and in Ireland, where it is sometimes found in the Gaelicized form Pléamonn.
German: variant of   Flemming  , cognate with 1 above.

variants.name: Flemings
meanings: English: plural form of   Fleming  .
Americanized form of Flemish Vleminckx: patronymic from Vleminck, an ethnic name for someone from Flanders, Middle Dutch vleminc.

variants.name: Flanders
meanings: English: ethnic name from Middle English Fla(u)ndres ‘Flanders’, denoting someone from Flanders, a historical region covering parts of modern-day Belgium, France, and the Netherlands. Compare Fleming .

variants.name: Fleeman
meanings: English: variant of   Fleming  .

variants.name: Flemming
meanings: German: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, Middle High German vlaeminc. People from Flanders spread throughout Germany, as well as England, in the Middle Ages as clothmakers and dyers.
English: variant of   Fleming  .

variants.name: Flamenco
meanings: Spanish: ethnic name from flamenco ‘Fleming, Flemish person’ (see Fleming ).

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Alard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gilles, Andre, Emile, Normand, Marcel, Alain, Armand, Gaston, Pierre, Antoine, Benoit, Fernand.

English: from a Middle English personal name, Alard, Adelard, Aylard, or Elard. The former two names are usually of native English origin, from Old English Æthelheard, composed of the elements æthel ‘noble’ + heard ‘hard, bold’. Alternatively, the surname may be of continental origin, from an ancient Germanic name (see 2 below), which developed in Old French to Alard, and in Middle Dutch to Adelaert and Alaert. The continental names were used in post-Conquest England by Normans and Flemings. Later development of -ard to -ett and -att in surnames means that these personal names may also lie behind some examples of e.g. Allatt and Hallett .
French: from the medieval personal name Alard, from ancient Germanic Adelhard, composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + hard ‘hardy’. Compare Allar , Allor , Allord , and Allore .

variants.name: Ellerd
meanings: English (Bedfordshire and Northamptonshire): variant of   Allard  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.
Probably an altered form of German Ellert, a variant of Ehlert.

variants.name: Ellert
meanings: Old German: Illustrious

variants.name: Allor
meanings: Hebrew: An oak.

variants.name: Hallett
meanings: English (mainly Somerset and Dorset): variant of Allatt, itself a variant of Allard , with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Ellard
meanings: English: variant of   Allard  .

variants.name: Allore
meanings: Americanized form of French   Allard  .

variants.name: Allord
meanings: Americanized form of French   Allard  .

en.lifeSummary: When Alard le Fleming II was born in 1220, in Sapperton, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Henry le Fleming I, was 21 and his mother, Joan, was 16. He married Emma Maunsell about 1240, in Pulborough, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died in 1263, in Pulborough, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 43, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alard le Fleming II est né en 1220, à Sapperton, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Henry le Fleming I, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Joan, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Emma Maunsell environ 1240, à Pulborough, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé en 1263, à Pulborough, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alard le Fleming II 1220 in Sapperton, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry le Fleming I 21 und seine Mutter Joan 16. Er hat um 1240 in Pulborough, Sussex, England, United Kingdom Emma Maunsell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb 1263 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Pulborough, Sussex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alard le Fleming II è nato il nel 1220 nel Sapperton, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Henry le Fleming I, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Joan, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Emma Maunsell circa 1240, nel Pulborough, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1263, nel Pulborough, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Alard le Fleming IIが1220年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアサップエルトンで生まれた時，父Henry le Fleming Iは21歳，母Joanは16歳であった。約1240年にイギリスイギリススセックスプウルボロウでEmma Maunsellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1263年にイギリスイギリススセックスプウルボロウで43歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alard le Fleming II(이)가 1220년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 사퍼턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry le Fleming I(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan(은)는 16세였다. 그는 대략 1240년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 풀버루그h에서 Emma Maunsell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1263년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 풀버루그h에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alard le Fleming II nasceu em 1220, em Sapperton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Henry le Fleming I, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Joan, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Emma Maunsell aproximadamente 1240, em Pulborough, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1263, em Pulborough, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alard le Fleming II родился в 1220 г. в местности саппиртон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Henry le Fleming I было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Joan, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Emma Maunsell около 1240 г. в местности пулбороую, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер в 1263 г. в местности пулбороую, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alard le Fleming II nació en 1220, en Sapperton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Henry le Fleming I, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Joan, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Emma Maunsell aproximadamente 1240, en Pulborough, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1263, en Pulborough, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
